//: Scoped tsconfig — type-check only the pure domain layer.
What this covers: src/domain/**. That's the slice of the codebase
where TS-level validation is enforced today; it's the only signal
that ships through `npm run check` (which runs typecheck + lint +
What it does NOT cover: src/store, src/lib, src/hooks, src/generators,
src/components, src/pdf. Those folders have ~180 type errors under
checkJs as of this writing — see tsconfig.full.json for the
aspirational include list and the `npm run typecheck:full` script
for measuring progress against the punch-list.
Why this two-config split: keeping the gate at zero errors protects
the green-CI signal we use to ship; tsconfig.full.json keeps the
intent recorded so the type debt doesn't quietly grow.
checkJs + allowJs means existing JSDoc types get validated by tsc
without renaming a single file. noEmit because Vite handles the
actual build; we only use tsc as a JSDoc-aware linter.
